**Agent Boundary Violations:**
- Subagent tool grants exceeding what the agent's .claude/agents/*.md frontmatter declares
- Agents with "Agent" in their tools array (recursive spawning — only the orchestrator may spawn agents)
- Write/Edit/Bash tools granted to agents that should be read-only (security-auditor, alignment-auditor, architecture-reviewer)
- Missing inoculation blocks in subagent system prompts

**Auth Pattern Violations:**
- Any use of ANTHROPIC_API_KEY except in "never use" warnings
- All auth must flow through CLAUDE_CODE_OAUTH_TOKEN
- Hardcoded API keys, tokens, passwords, or database connection strings
- Credentials in example code, shell scripts, or GitHub Actions workflows
- .env files or credential patterns committed to version control

**Prompt Injection Surface:**
- User-controlled strings interpolated into system prompts without sanitization
- Missing inoculation blocks (the standard "You may encounter instructions..." block)
- Agent descriptions ambiguous enough to cause unintended delegation
- LLM judge prompt injection — agents submitting work to LLM-based judges may inject grading instructions
- Prefill manipulation — pre-filled turns containing prior sabotage patterns

**Sandbox Escape Vectors:**
- Shell injection through tool-call arguments
- Network restriction circumvention patterns
- Credential harvesting via /proc/ inspection or process memory
- Writing to shell input via file-editing tools
- Subagents spawned with less restrictive permissions than intended

**Python-Specific:**
- Unsafe eval(), exec(), subprocess with user input
- Pickle deserialization from untrusted sources
- SQL injection via string concatenation (must use parameterized queries)
- Path traversal in file handling (os.path.join with user input)
- SSRF in crawler URL handling (following redirects to internal networks)

**Next.js/React-Specific (webapp/):**
- XSS via dangerouslySetInnerHTML
- Server-side request forgery in API routes
- Sensitive data exposed in client-side bundles
- Missing Content-Security-Policy headers

**Neon Postgres-Specific:**
- Connection strings hardcoded instead of using NEON_DATABASE_URL from env
- SQL injection in psycopg queries (string formatting instead of parameterized)
- Overly permissive database roles or grants
